verification_enforcement.rationale: Decision-Maker may perform bounded independent source discovery/search during targeted escalation, but only within runtime-enforced tool/query/fetch budgets.

prose_fallback.decision_handoff_excerpt:
# Decision summary

Javokhir Sindarov is very likely to win the 2026 FIDE Candidates Tournament, but the best post-synthesis estimate is still slightly below the 95.05% market because the strongest independently checked evidence supports an overwhelming late lead rather than a fully settled official result.

## Why this may matter now

Market-implied probability is 0.9505. My syndicated range is 0.89 to 0.94. That is still a strong yes view, but the edge versus market is marginal-to-negative rather than actionable. The market may be a touch rich because it is pricing a near-converted lead almost like an already-declared win.

## Shift versus swarm baseline

This is not a large move from the swarm-implied center around 0.91. I stay close to it because the strongest lanes were broadly right: the visible state is not generic player-strength optimism but a specific late-event lead. I lean slightly toward the upper half of the swarm because the synthesis-stage check supported the key catalyst-hunter claim that Sindarov was two points clear and at least playoff-secured. I do not move up to market because direct official standings verification remained imperfect.

## Edge verification sta …

selected_persona_sidecars.main_thesis: The market is directionally right because Sindarov appears to have an almost decisive late-stage lead, but 95.05% is slightly too high given residual two-round, tiebreak, and official-live-verification risk.
main_logical_chain: Start from the 95.05% market price as a serious prior.
Ask what would justify that price: an overwhelming late-stage live lead rather than generic pre-event dominance.
Contextual standings indicate exactly that state, with Sindarov on 9/12 and nearest chaser on 7/12 after 12 rounds.
Because FIDE is the resolution authority and two rounds remain, trim modestly for residual conversion and verification risk.
strongest_supports: Contextual live standings show Sindarov leading by two points with two rounds left.
The tournament is only 14 rounds, so there are few remaining opportunities for the field to catch up.
strongest_disconfirmers: Two rounds plus possible tie-breaks still leave a nonzero failure path.
Official FIDE live standings were not cleanly extracted in this run, so some confidence relies on contextual sources.
unresolved_ambiguities: Exact official live standings were not directly captured from a clean FIDE page in this run.
Residual tie scenarios depend on remaining round outcomes not yet played.
selected_persona_sidecars.what_would_change_view: A clean official FIDE standings/result update showing a smaller lead, a round-13 stumble, or any procedural dispute would push the estimate down materially.
selected_persona_sidecars.timing_relevance: Very high; this is a late-stage in-progress tournament market where one round can change the entire tail-risk profile.
selected_persona_sidecars.source_quality_view: Resolution authority is high-quality and clear via FIDE, while the live-state evidence is strong but more contextual than ideal, so overall source quality is medium-high rather than fully high.

selected_persona_sidecars.main_thesis: Sindarov is a real contender, but the visible evidence does not justify the market's 95.05% near-certainty absent hidden live-state context.
main_logical_chain: Official FIDE source confirms Sindarov is legitimately qualified and the event format/winner logic are clear.
That same source shows a deep field and long format, so baseline tournament uncertainty remains substantial.
Because the market is pricing 95.05%, extra verification is required.
Extra verification did not uncover public evidence in this run that makes the outcome nearly settled.
strongest_supports: FIDE officially lists Sindarov in the 2026 Candidates field and as 2025 World Cup winner.
Polymarket rules designate official FIDE information as primary resolution source.
strongest_disconfirmers: The same FIDE page shows a full elite eight-player double round-robin, which is hard to square with 95% for one player absent hidden context.
This run did not verify standings or rival ineligibility that would rationalize near-certainty.
unresolved_ambiguities: Whether the market snapshot reflects late-event state not visible in fetched sources.
Whether cross-runner pricing is coherent or structurally distorted.
selected_persona_sidecars.what_would_change_view: Official FIDE standings showing Sindarov with a commanding late lead, rival ineligibility, or strong independent odds near 95% would move me toward the market.
selected_persona_sidecars.timing_relevance: High; if the event is already underway or nearly finished, standings could materially change the interpretation of the 95.05% price.
selected_persona_sidecars.source_quality_view: Authoritative source-of-truth on field and rules is strong, but independent contextual verification was weaker than ideal due to search-tool failure.

selected_persona_sidecars.main_thesis: Sindarov is very likely to win, but the market is slightly overconfident because the tournament is not yet officially settled and residual tiebreak/admin tails still exist.
main_logical_chain: The contract resolves on an actual FIDE-declared winner, not merely on a dominant in-progress position.
Secondary sources indicate Sindarov is in a hugely favorable late-stage position.
Because two rounds and possible tiebreaks still remain, residual competitive and administrative tails are not zero.
Therefore the right view is strongly yes, but slightly below the 95.05% market price.
strongest_supports: Contextual standings show Sindarov leading 9/12 by two points with two rounds left.
Chess.com round-12 coverage says he is guaranteed at least a playoff.
strongest_disconfirmers: A two-point lead with two rounds left in this format may make 95% entirely fair or even slightly low if standings are accurate.
The bearish case is mostly about tail-risk pricing rather than a plausible mainline loss narrative.
unresolved_ambiguities: Exact official live standings/tiebreak math were not reconstructed from a primary FIDE event page.
Low-probability off-board complications remain hard to rule out completely.
selected_persona_sidecars.what_would_change_view: An official FIDE confirmation that Sindarov has clinched or clean official standings with no complications would push the estimate up; contradictory official reporting or a sharply narrowed lead would push it down.
selected_persona_sidecars.timing_relevance: Very high; this is a late-stage tournament market where each remaining round can move the probability sharply.
selected_persona_sidecars.source_quality_view: Good enough for a high-confidence directional yes, but not perfect for a 95%+ certainty claim because live-state verification relied mainly on secondary reporting.

frontmatter.topic: market confidence versus field strength in 2026 FIDE Candidates
frontmatter.question: Will Javokhir Sindarov win the 2026 FIDE Candidates Tournament?

selected_assumption_snippets.snippet:
# Assumption
The market's 95.05% price is implicitly assuming either that Javokhir Sindarov is an overwhelming pre-tournament favorite versus seven elite qualifiers or that substantial contract/listing context outside the visible rules makes his victory close to settled.
## Why this assumption matters
Without that assumption, a pre-event price above 95% in an eight-player double round-robin is extremely hard to justify. The whole risk-manager view turns on whether this confidence comes from real hidden information or from market mispricing / market-structure distortion.
## What this assumption supports
- A view that the displayed market price is likely too confident.
- A probability estimate materially below the market-implied baseline.
- Elevated concern about hidden assumptions, field-strength underweighting, or book-structure issues.
## Evidence or logic behind the assumption
- Official FIDE page confirms a full eight-player field rather than a one-opponent match.
- The format is long and variance-exposed: 14 rounds plus playoff if tied.
- The official field includes multiple world-class players, so the base-rate path to 95% for any one player appears implausible absent extraordinary form or unseen disqualifying constraints.
- Polymarket rules do not show any special shortcut that would make Sindarov nearly locked absent actual tournament play.
## What would falsify it
- Evidence that this market is being quoted after the tournament has largely been played and Sindarov already leads by an almost insurmountable margin.
- Official evidence that several listed rivals are no …
